sample.title: Why Gen Z Is Gambling With Its Future
sample.description:
With homeownership out of reach, artificial intelligence cutting job prospects and index funds feeling too slow, many young investors are turning to meme stocks, options and crypto to meet their financial goals. 

Read more on Bloomberg.com: 

Gen-Z Traders Go for Broke in Pursuit of a New American Dream
https://www.bloomberg.com/news/features/2026-06-17/ai-meme-stock-gains-shape-gen-z-s-embrace-of-risk-assets

00:00 Introduction
01:51 Risk mindset
03:49 ‘Financial Nihilism’
05:47 Gamification of trading
06:38 Alternative investments 
08:43 Misunderstanding Gen Z

sample.title: Energy Prices Ease In June, Dropping U.S. Inflation To 3.5% Annually
sample.description:
The June consumer price index shows that annual inflation is moving closer to the Federal Reserve’s 2% target. Energy put downward pressure on inflation as a lull in the Iran war easing prices for consumers. CNBC’s Alex Harring breaks down what the June CPI reveals about prices.

sample.title: How China came to dominate the global EV factory boom
sample.description:
Chinese automakers have made over $100 billion in EV and battery investments abroad since 2019, according to Atlas Public Policy, far outpacing US firms. CNBC’s Robert Ferris has more on how the Asian country got here and why.

sample.title: Americans keep getting priced out of fun. Here's how it impacts us at home
sample.description:
"Funflation," the rising cost of concerts, sporting events, and travel, is surging across America. Now, even staying at home and doing activities like gaming and streaming is expensive. That's because of tariffs, a global memory shortage, and fewer customers. CNBC’s Alex Harring has the details.

Reporting by: Alex Harring

sample.title: Will The New Housing Bill Lower Housing Costs?
sample.description:
The largest housing bill in decades has just become law after weeks of tense political standoff between President Trump and Congress. The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act focuses on three major reforms. It aims to increase the supply of housing by combining federal incentives for new homes with reforms designed to speed up home construction. The law also seeks to expand access to housing finance while limiting large institutional investors from buying single-family homes. Now comes the real test: can this ambitious legislation actually make housing affordable again?

Credits:
Reporter: Sarah Agostino, Mike Winters
Producer: Juhohn Lee
Editor: Meline Rosales
Animation: Jason Reginato, Emily Park
Senior Director of Video: Jessica Leibowitz
Additional Footage: Getty Images

sample.title: How Ukraine's drone strikes are choking off Russian shipping
sample.description:
Drones operated by the Ukrainian Special Operations Forces’ “Birds” unit had targeted 116 vessels in the Sea of Azov over the past nine days, according to Ukrainian serviceman Major Robert Brovdi. The attacks have forced Russia to restrict travel through the pivotal Kerch Strait and rethink how it safely transports its fuel and commodities. Sean Hogan explains. 
 
#russia #ukraine #drones #attack #shipping

sample.title: How the Esports World Cup was built in 8 weeks | BBC News
sample.description:
The Esports World Cup has arrived in Paris for the first time, bringing one of the world's biggest competitive gaming events to the French capital.
 
BBC journalist Andrew Rogers was given exclusive behind-the-scenes access as the competition got underway.
 
Esports Foundation CEO Mike McCabe explains how organisers transformed a venue into a world-class esports arena in just eight weeks, switching out of Riyadh for the first time. The event was moved because of ongoing conflict in the region, although bosses say the EWC will return to Saudi Arabia in 2027.
 
With a combined prize pool of $75m (£55m), more than 2,000 competitors and millions expected to watch around the world, the event is one of the largest in professional gaming.
 
Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news
 
#EsportsWorldCup #Esports #LeagueOfLegends #Valorant #BBCGaming #BBCNews

sample.title: UK climate extremes becoming 'new normal', says Met Office | BBC News
sample.description:
Weather that was once considered extreme is becoming the "new normal", according to the latest Met Office report on the UK's climate.
 
It shows the hottest day of the year in the south of England is now typically 4.5C warmer than it was in 1961-1990 and that the last four years are in the top five warmest on record for the UK.
 
The climate we had in the 20th Century has "now gone", according to the report's lead author Mike Kendon.

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#Weather #UK #Climate #BBCNews
